blocks.content: The fence looked different in the morning.

At night everything condemned had a certain appeal. In the morning it just looked old and tired — chain-link leaning in two places, the condemned sign bleached to pale coral, the house beyond it two stories of silver-gray wood with the porch sagging on one end.
They locked their bikes to the fence. Daisy and Savannah locked theirs. Kit and Eli leaned theirs against the chain-link.
Daisy looked at Kit's bike. Then at Kit.
Kit held the fence gap open so Daisy could get through without catching her shirt on the edge, and she went through without comment.
The yard was bigger than it looked from outside. A rusted truck sat on its rims, a small tree growing straight up through its bed.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: You know the best part? Everyone thought I was making up the saddle.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: You were making up the parts you couldn't verify.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: That's different from making it up.
They crossed the yard toward the barn, the grass swishing against their shins.
